issues.body: After the initial trust step succeeds, `Mark trusted live issue in progress` calls `status --status in-progress`. `set_status` removes `status:ready` and adds `status:in-progress`. Much later, `republish-trust` invokes the unchanged `reverify`, which requires `status:ready` and rejects `status:in-progress` through `BUSY_OR_GATED`. No intervening step restores ready. Thus `republish-trust.outcome` is always failure on the normal live path, and the newly gated `pr` step is always skipped. This makes publication completely non-functional rather than providing a usable last-step trust check.
issues.fix_hint: Add a dedicated pre-publish verification mode that recognizes exactly this run's still-valid in-progress claim while remaining fail-closed against another run, human parking, or other busy/gated states (for example, bind the accepted state to an immutable claim/run receipt). Do not simply allow all status:in-progress issues. Add an end-to-end/self-test covering the actual transition: ready -> this run's in-progress claim -> pre-publish reverify succeeds, while a mismatched claim or human gate fails.
issues.title: Pre-publish reverify always rejects the worker's own in-progress state

issues.body: The new assertions prove that the publish step references republish-trust and that the verifier is pinned, but they would all pass while republish-trust deterministically rejects every real run because the issue is already status:in-progress. The missing assertion is that pre-publish revalidation succeeds after the workflow's own ready-to-in-progress transition for the same claim, and fails for a foreign or unbound in-progress claim.
issues.fix_hint: Add a non-vacuous test that models the workflow label lifecycle and invokes the pre-publish reverify path: the current run's bound in-progress state must pass, while status:in-progress without the matching current-run claim must fail.
issues.title: Self-tests omit the live label-state transition required by republish-trust

verdict.summary: The immutable verifier work addresses the prior round's trust-root findings, but the new publish-time reverify cannot succeed in the actual workflow. Before the model runs, the workflow transitions the issue from status:ready to status:in-progress; reverify still requires status:ready and also treats status:in-progress as a blocker. Consequently every otherwise successful live run aborts at republish-trust and never publishes. The added workflow self-tests validate wiring and verifier provenance but do not exercise this label-state lifecycle. Because this is a new blocker despite resolution of the prior findings, progress is regressing under the required scale.
